metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a cardinality assertion to the AC7 proving command so it fails when no matching commit is found.
evidence: git log --grep with no matching commit returns exit code 0 and produces no output — the command cannot distinguish 'commit found' from 'commit absent'
VERIFY-COMMAND DEFECT TAXONOMY type (5): missing cardinality assertion when the criterion implies at least one result must be present
findings.finding: AC7's git log proving command exits 0 with empty output when the commit is absent, so it silently passes without the change actually landing on main.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — final bullet: `git log origin/main --oneline --grep='rebar-ticket: 9447-b31c-e0b7-446d'`
scenarios: An agent or CI job runs the command after a failed/missing merge, sees no output, treats exit-0 as success, and marks the story complete without the change on main.
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace the proving command with:
`git log origin/main --oneline --grep='rebar-ticket: 9447-b31c-e0b7-446d' | grep -c '' | xargs -I{} test {} -ge 1`
or more simply:
`git log origin/main --oneline --grep='rebar-ticket: 9447-b31c-e0b7-446d' | grep -q .`
Either form exits non-zero when the commit is absent.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the reference to 'executed-criteria coverage/batch plan' with the actual sidecar field(s) that record which criteria ran (e.g., the finding's `criteria: ["project.review-phase-boundaries"]` entry in the `advisory` bucket), or confirm that dependency 2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02 introduces such a field and name it explicitly.
evidence: Search for 'executed-criteria' across entire repo: no matches
Search for 'coverage/batch plan' across entire repo: no matches
Code-review sidecar schema defined in `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py` (SCHEMA = 'code_review_result_v2') — no 'coverage' or 'batch plan' field

The sidecar does carry `blocking`, `advisory`, `dropped`, `indeterminate` buckets (referenced later in the same Preconditions paragraph and confirmed in `test_audit_code_review_v2.py`), but not an 'executed-criteria' field
findings.finding: The plan's 'acceptable review trace' definition asserts the sidecar JSON must show 'the physical id `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries` in the executed-criteria coverage/batch plan', but no field or structure named 'coverage/batch plan' or 'executed-criteria' exists in the code-review sidecar schema, any emitted sidecar payload, or any codebase source file.
scenarios: An operator reviewing a live sidecar trace cannot locate the 'executed-criteria coverage/batch plan' field because it does not exist in the current schema, making the acceptability criterion impossible to verify as written.
If the field is introduced by dependency 2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02 (the seam story), that assumption is implicit and unverifiable from the plan text.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify how the operator verifies that physical id `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries` ran: the code-review sidecar does not carry `batch_plan` (only `coverage: {llm_ran: true}`); either update the AC to name the actual sidecar field that proves criterion execution, or specify an alternative artifact (e.g. bot message text, workflow step log, or the `reviewer_id` field on the finding) as the ground-truth evidence.
evidence: Plan Preconditions: 'show the physical id `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries` in the executed-criteria coverage/batch plan'

sidecar.build_payload (sidecar.py:94-112) keys: schema, impact_model_version, verdict, ticket_id, change_id, revision, change_fingerprint, session_id, deps, runner, model, coverage, blocking, advisory, dropped, indeterminate, coaching — no batch_plan
CodeReviewBatchRunner.run (batch_runner.py:83): outputs={'findings': ..., 'criteria_count': ..., 'batch_plan': plan} — batch_plan not forwarded to sidecar
code_review_coach (workflow_ops.py:502-510): coverage = ctx.inputs.get('coverage') or {'llm_ran': True} — no ran[] list
The workflow interpreter (interpreter.py:393-394) explicitly calls batch_plan OPAQUE and never inspects it
findings.finding: The plan's Preconditions and AC specify verifying the physical id `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries` in the 'executed-criteria coverage/batch plan' of the review sidecar JSON, but the code-review sidecar (`sidecar.build_payload` in `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py:94-112`) does NOT contain a `batch_plan` field. The `batch_plan` (which holds `{'ran': [...physical_prompt_ids...]}`) is produced by `CodeReviewBatchRunner.run` and stored opaquely in the workflow interpreter step state; it is never propagated to the sidecar. The sidecar carries only `coverage` (set to `{'llm_ran': True}` by `code_review_coach`), which does not enumerate the physical prompt ids that ran. As written, the AC is unverifiable from the sidecar alone.
findings.location: Preconditions section ('show the physical id … in the executed-criteria coverage/batch plan') and AC for negative-probe patchset
scenarios: Operator attempts to verify from the sidecar JSON that the project criterion ran against the reviewed SHA; the sidecar has no field listing which physical prompt ids were executed, so the attestation requirement cannot be satisfied as specified.
A future auditor inspecting the sidecar trace cannot confirm from the sidecar alone that `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries` was included in the batch run.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'show the physical id … in the executed-criteria coverage/batch plan' with 'the advisory finding in the sidecar carries `reviewer_id: "code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries"`, confirming the physical criterion ran and emitted the finding'. This grounds the verification in the `reviewer_id` field that `CodeReviewBatchRunner` stamps on every finding (batch_runner.py:79) and that is preserved in the sidecar's `advisory[]` list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the undefined 'executed-criteria coverage/batch plan' field reference in Preconditions with a field path that actually exists in the code-review sidecar (e.g., verify the physical id appears in the `advisory[*].criteria` or `advisory[*].reviewer_id` of the sidecar, or confirm `coverage.llm_ran == true` combined with the criterion id appearing in a finding's `criteria` list), and update AC3 and AC4 to match the corrected field path.
evidence: Plan Preconditions: 'It must contain the reviewed revision SHA, show the physical id `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries` in the executed-criteria coverage/batch plan, and retain the `blocking`, `advisory`, `dropped`, and `indeterminate` verdict lists.'
src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py build_payload(): coverage is read as `verdict.get('coverage', {})` — whatever `_attach_code_review_metrics` wrote, never a batch_plan subfield

src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py _attach_code_review_metrics(): writes coverage['llm_ran'], coverage['metrics'], coverage['grounding_note'], coverage['approach_viability_note'] — no batch_plan or executed-criteria

src/rebar/llm/code_review/batch_runner.py line 83: `BatchRunResult(outputs={'findings': findings, 'criteria_count': ..., 'batch_plan': plan})` — batch_plan stays in workflow step outputs, never forwarded to verdict coverage
src/rebar/schemas/code_review_verdict.schema.json coverage property: 'Run coverage: {llm_ran, metrics:{llm_ms,total_ms,llm_calls,...}}' — no batch_plan field defined
findings.finding: The Preconditions section specifies that the acceptable sidecar trace must 'show the physical id `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries` in the executed-criteria coverage/batch plan,' but the code-review sidecar schema has no 'executed-criteria' or 'batch_plan' subfield under `coverage`. The `batch_plan` object (which contains `ran: [prompt_ids...]`) is produced by `CodeReviewBatchRunner.run()` and lives in `BatchRunResult.outputs` but is never persisted into the sidecar's `coverage` dict — confirmed by `sidecar.build_payload()` (which reads `verdict.get('coverage', {})`) and `_attach_code_review_metrics()` (which writes only `llm_ran`, `metrics`, and advisory notes into `coverage`). The field path the plan uses as its observable proxy for criterion execution does not exist in the artifact operators are directed to inspect.
findings.location: Preconditions section: 'show the physical id `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries` in the executed-criteria coverage/batch plan'
scenarios: After both dependency stories land and the probe runs, an operator inspects the sidecar JSON to verify AC3/AC4 by locating the physical id in 'coverage/batch_plan' — that path does not exist in the artifact. The operator cannot satisfy the AC as written and the story cannot be attested.

If the plan is amended to look at the `ran` list inside `batch_plan`, this only exists in the in-memory workflow step recorder output, not the persisted sidecar — meaning it requires a different verification approach (e.g., inspecting the Gerrit bot message or a separate workflow log) rather than the sidecar JSON.
findings.suggested_fix: In Preconditions, replace 'show the physical id `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries` in the executed-criteria coverage/batch plan' with 'show at least one finding whose `criteria` list contains `project.review-phase-boundaries` (negative probe AC4) or confirm `coverage.llm_ran == true` with no such finding (positive-control AC3), using the sidecar's `advisory`, `blocking`, `dropped`, and `indeterminate` arrays as the evidence surface.' Update AC3 and AC4 accordingly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the Preconditions section's sidecar-retrieval description: remove 'linked from the Gerrit bot message' as an acquisition path (the voter posts only a text vote; no sidecar link appears in the Gerrit message) and replace it with the operationally valid path — the operator retrieves the sidecar from the server's ambient tickets store (the `code_review` artifact ticket whose title is `code-review: <change_id> @ <revision>`) and pastes it into a ticket comment.
evidence: voter.py line 614-615: gc.post_vote(change_id, revision, value, message) posts only the vote value + text message to Gerrit
voter.py lines 662-668: emit_code_review_artifact called with repo_root=None after the vote; sidecar written to ambient tickets store, not to Gerrit change
adapter.py lines 64-74: _message_tag returns '[LLM-Review: …]' text only; no sidecar URL or link is included
No code path in voter.py or adapter.py attaches or references the artifact ticket id in the Gerrit vote message
findings.finding: The plan asserts the acceptable review trace can be obtained 'linked from the Gerrit bot message,' but the voter code (voter.py) only posts a plain text `[LLM-Review: …]` vote and inline robot_comments to Gerrit — it never appends a sidecar link to the posted message. The sidecar is written to the server's ambient tickets store via emit_code_review_artifact (repo_root=None), not to the Gerrit change. An operator following the 'linked from the Gerrit bot message' acquisition path will find nothing there.
findings.location: Preconditions section: 'the review sidecar JSON, either linked from the Gerrit bot message or pasted into a ticket comment'
scenarios: An operator completing this story searches the Gerrit change's bot message for a sidecar link, finds none, and either incorrectly concludes the bot is misconfigured or spends time investigating the wrong place.

The 'linked from the Gerrit bot message' option is listed first and may be read as the primary retrieval path, obscuring that 'pasted into a ticket comment' (manual extraction from the tickets store) is the only operationally available path.
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'the review sidecar JSON, either linked from the Gerrit bot message or pasted into a ticket comment' to 'the review sidecar JSON, retrieved from the server's ambient tickets store (the `code_review` artifact ticket with title `code-review: <change_id> @ <revision>`, written by `emit_code_review_artifact`) and pasted into a ticket comment on this ticket.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove or correct the 'linked from the Gerrit bot message' sidecar-access option: the current voter does not link or embed the sidecar in the Gerrit review message; specify the accurate retrieval path (operator reads the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar from the bot host's rebar ticket store and pastes it as a ticket comment).
evidence: voter.py emit_code_review_artifact: sidecar.emit(verdict, target_ticket=artifact_id, repo_root=None, ...) — emits to the local ticket store only, with repo_root=None (the ambient host store, not the Gerrit change)
voter.py post_vote (line ~615): gc.post_vote(change_id, revision, value, message) — the message is decision.get('message'), not a sidecar link; no sidecar URL is included
The Gerrit bot message format shown in tests (test_review_bot.py): '[LLM-Review: PASS]' / '[LLM-Review: BLOCK — finding]' — no sidecar link
Evidence class: CONTRADICTED — the 'linked from Gerrit bot message' path is falsified by voter.py
findings.finding: The plan offers 'linked from the Gerrit bot message' as one of two paths to obtain the sidecar JSON for the acceptable review trace. The voter code (voter.py emit_code_review_artifact) shows the sidecar is stored only to the rebar local ticket store — it is never included in or linked from the Gerrit bot message. An operator attempting this path will find it does not exist; only the 'pasted into a ticket comment' branch is feasible.
findings.location: Preconditions section — 'the review sidecar JSON, either linked from the Gerrit bot message or pasted into a ticket comment'
scenarios: An operator running the probe looks for a sidecar link in the Gerrit change's LLM-Review message and finds none, causing confusion about how to obtain the required trace.

The precondition check for 'an acceptable review trace' is delayed while the operator searches for a non-existent Gerrit-side link, when the correct path is to read from the bot host's rebar ticket store.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'either linked from the Gerrit bot message or pasted into a ticket comment' with 'pasted into a ticket comment (the operator retrieves the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar payload from the rebar ticket store on the review-bot host, keyed to the code_review artifact for the relevant change_id + revision).'

evidence: 2 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
vague/subjective terms present: clean
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace `.venv/bin/python` in the routing-verification AC with a platform-agnostic invocation (e.g. `python` after activating the venv, or `python -m ...` via an activation step) so the command works on both POSIX and Windows.
evidence: `.venv/bin/python -c 'import json; r=json.load(open(".rebar/criteria_routing.json"))["code_review"]["project.review-phase-boundaries"]; assert r["block_threshold"] == 0.90 and r["blocking_enabled"] is False'`
On Windows, `python -m venv .venv` places the interpreter at `.venv\Scripts\python.exe`, not `.venv/bin/python`; the POSIX path is absent.
Windows is a named platform in the support matrix: 'macOS, Windows, Linux, BSD, CI, servers, developer workstations'.
findings.finding: The AC verification command `.venv/bin/python -c '...'` uses a POSIX virtualenv path that does not exist on Windows, where the Python executable lives at `.venv\Scripts\python.exe`. An operator running this AC on a Windows workstation or Windows CI runner will receive a 'no such file or directory' error and be unable to verify the routing assertion.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — second bullet: `verify with .venv/bin/python -c 'import json; ...'`
scenarios: Windows developer workstation: operator copies the AC verification command verbatim; shell reports `'.venv/bin/python' is not recognized` or `No such file or directory`; AC cannot be confirmed green, blocking story sign-off on that platform.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the hardcoded path with a two-step form: first activate the venv (`source .venv/bin/activate` on POSIX / `.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1` on Windows, or just rely on `python` being on PATH after activation), then run `python -c 'import json; ...'`. Alternatively, use `python -c` with the venv already activated, and document the activation step as a prerequisite.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Unify the artifact label: pick one term (e.g. 'acceptable sidecar trace') and replace all variants, or add a back-reference in AC3/AC4/AC5 such as 'acceptable sidecar trace (as defined in Preconditions)' so the full requirements (SHA, physical id, verdict lists) are unambiguously in scope for every operator-attested AC.
evidence: Preconditions: 'an acceptable review trace is the review sidecar JSON'
Scope: 'Confirm the project-only physical prompt id ran against the reviewed repository SHA using the acceptable sidecar trace'
AC3/AC4/AC5: each references 'an acceptable sidecar trace' without back-linking to the Preconditions definition
findings.finding: Three distinct labels are used for the same artifact across sections — 'acceptable review trace' (Preconditions, definition site), 'review sidecar JSON' (Preconditions, same paragraph), and 'acceptable sidecar trace' (Scope, all three operator-attested ACs) — creating terminological drift that could cause an operator to question whether these are the same thing or subtly different deliverables.
findings.location: Preconditions (definition); Scope ('acceptable sidecar trace'); AC3, AC4, AC5 ('acceptable sidecar trace')
scenarios: An operator auditing AC3 searches for 'acceptable sidecar trace' and does not immediately find its definition, leading to ambiguity about whether the Preconditions' more detailed requirements (SHA, physical id, verdict lists) apply to all three ACs or only to the one explicitly named in Preconditions.
findings.suggested_fix: In Preconditions, rename 'an acceptable review trace is the review sidecar JSON' to 'An acceptable sidecar trace is the review sidecar JSON, either linked from the Gerrit bot message or pasted into a ticket comment.' Then all downstream uses of 'acceptable sidecar trace' are self-consistent.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit timeout window (e.g., 'if no `LLM-Review` message appears within N hours of patchset upload, declare the bot absent and follow the operational-bug path') so operators have a deterministic escalation trigger.
evidence: Plan text: 'If the bot is absent or misconfigured, do not substitute an offline run: create an operational bug linked `discovered_from`, mark this story blocked'
ABSENCE: no timeout duration or polling interval is specified for waiting on the bot to post its `LLM-Review` result
The bot-slow scenario (e.g., review job queued but delayed hours) is not distinguished from bot-absent
findings.finding: The plan specifies what to do when the bot is 'absent or misconfigured' but defines no timeout or wait-window after which a slow-but-unresponsive bot is declared absent. An operator could wait indefinitely, with no guidance on when to escalate to the 'create operational bug, mark blocked' path.
findings.location: Preconditions section: 'If the bot is absent or misconfigured, do not substitute an offline run…'
scenarios: Deployed bot is healthy but backlogged; operator waits indefinitely before declaring it absent
Bot posts the review after operator has already raised a false-positive operational bug and marked the story blocked
findings.suggested_fix: In the Preconditions section, add: 'Allow up to [X] hours after patchset upload for the bot to post its LLM-Review result. If no result appears within that window, treat the bot as absent: create an operational bug linked `discovered_from`, mark this story blocked, and do not proceed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a dependency-landed guard to AC2 (e.g., note that the command is only valid after `225a-323c-a9f6-436b` lands and the `code_review.project.review-phase-boundaries` key is present), or adjust the command to fail with a clear message when the key is absent.
evidence: Current `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` content: `{"plan_review": {"project.portability": {...}}, "activate": ["project.portability"]}` — no `code_review` key present
AC2 command: `r=json.load(open(".rebar/criteria_routing.json"))["code_review"]["project.review-phase-boundaries"]` — `["code_review"]` lookup fails on current file

Dependency ticket 225a-323c-a9f6-436b attestation records: `dep b199211117f28cfabfb0565e43295a87b2f79d4d0c0d9e3bcc237e98c2957be1 .rebar/criteria_routing.json` (the file exists but without the code_review section)
Plan cache finding `c5d1db965c5326d3448a.json`: 'The AC routing-verification command will raise a `KeyError` if run before dependency `225a-323c-a9f6-436b` has landed'
findings.finding: The AC2 routing-verification command presupposes `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` has a `code_review.project.review-phase-boundaries` key, but reading the actual file shows only a `plan_review` section with `project.portability` — no `code_review` key exists. The command will raise `KeyError` on the current (and any pre-dependency-landing) repo state.
scenarios: Anyone running the AC2 verification command against the repo before dependency 225a-323c-a9f6-436b lands gets a Python KeyError, not an assertion failure, making the check silent-fail rather than a clear gate.
If AC2 is run as part of a CI gate or handoff checklist before the dependency is merged, the story appears to fail for an infrastructure reason rather than a routing misconfiguration.
findings.suggested_fix: Prefix the AC2 command with a check: `python -c 'import json; d=json.load(open(".rebar/criteria_routing.json")); assert "code_review" in d and "project.review-phase-boundaries" in d["code_review"], "dependency 225a-323c-a9f6-436b not yet landed"; ...'`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit Preconditions check that both `.rebar/prompts/code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.md` and `.rebar/evals/code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.eval.yaml` exist in the repo before beginning the probe (e.g., verify with `ls .rebar/prompts/code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.md .rebar/evals/code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.eval.yaml`).
evidence: `.rebar/prompts/` directory listing: only `plan-review-project-portability.md` — `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.md` is absent
`.rebar/evals/` directory listing: only `plan-review-project-portability.eval.yaml` — `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.eval.yaml` is absent
Dependency ticket 225a-323c-a9f6-436b attestation records both as 'dep absent', confirming they are outputs of that story which is still open
This plan's own dependency section correctly lists 225a-323c-a9f6-436b as a precondition, but the plan body treats the probe as runnable without confirming those files have landed
findings.finding: The plan asserts `.rebar/prompts/code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.md` and `.rebar/evals/code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.eval.yaml` exist (implicitly, by requiring the probe to run the criterion), but both files are currently absent from the repository.
findings.location: Preconditions / What — probe design; Scope — 'Confirm the project-only physical prompt id ran'
scenarios: If this story's precondition check is performed before 225a-323c-a9f6-436b closes, the probe will fail with a fatal `LLMError` (per the seam story's own spec: 'an activated missing or malformed asset is a fatal configuration failure') rather than producing a reviewable sidecar trace.
The story could be marked 'operator-attested' prematurely if the operator doesn't verify the files exist before running the probe.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Preconditions: 'Before initiating any patchset, confirm both `.rebar/prompts/code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.md` and `.rebar/evals/code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.eval.yaml` are present on `main`; verify with `ls .rebar/prompts/code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.md .rebar/evals/code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.eval.yaml`'.

findings.finding: The Preconditions and AC sections assert the trace 'must name… the number of that criterion's candidates forwarded to Pass 2 and Pass 3' and 'resolved prompt path or content hash', but the actual sidecar payload (sidecar.py `build_payload`) contains no per-criterion candidate-forwarding counts and no resolved prompt path field — only post-Pass-3 verdict lists (blocking/advisory/dropped/indeterminate). The plan assumes a trace artifact exists that carries these fields, but they are not emitted by any current code path.
findings.location: Preconditions section: 'It must name `project.review-phase-boundaries`, the reviewed repository SHA, the repository-local resolved prompt path or content hash, and the number of that criterion's candidates forwarded to Pass 2 and Pass 3'; AC lines 3 and 4

findings.finding: The plan requires an 'acceptable review trace' that names `project.review-phase-boundaries`, the reviewed repository SHA, the repository-local resolved prompt path or content hash, and per-criterion candidate counts forwarded to Pass 2 and Pass 3 — but whether the deployed review bot's log or export today actually emits these specific structured fields is unverified.
findings.location: Preconditions section and AC (negative-probe and positive-control operator-attested ACs)

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
